{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Narita-san Temple]][[en:draft:Narita-san Temple]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Narita-san Temple|Narita-san Temple]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺}} in [[Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺}} is a [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Chizan Sect|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|ja|犬山市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|ja|成田市}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|ja|1936年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year }} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|ja|松坂屋}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Fudō Myō-ō|ja|不動明王}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|ja|名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008年12月27日}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり}} * {{ill|Kanbutsu-e|ja|灌仏会}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Bon Festival|ja|お盆}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|ja|犬山城}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|ja|名鉄広見線}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|ja|名鉄小牧線}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|ja|犬山駅}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Yūen Station|ja|犬山遊園駅}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokiwayama Stable|ja|時津風部屋}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|ja|時津風部屋力士暴行死事件}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|ja|狭山不動尊}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.

== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|ja|名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり}} * {{ill|Kanbutsu-e|ja|灌仏会}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Bon Festival|ja|お盆}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|ja|犬山城}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|ja|名鉄広見線}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|ja|名鉄小牧線}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|ja|犬山駅}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Yūen Station|ja|犬山遊園駅}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokiwayama Stable|ja|時津風部屋}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|ja|時津風部屋力士暴行死事件}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|ja|狭山不動尊}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺}} in [[Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺}} is a [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Chizan Sect|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|ja|犬山市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|ja|成田市}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|ja|1936th year }} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year }} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|ja|松坂屋}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Fudō Myō-ō|ja|不動明王}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|ja|名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり}} * {{ill|Kanbutsu-e|ja|灌仏会}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Bon Festival|ja|お盆}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|ja|犬山城}} from the Main Hall }}

== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokiwayama Stable|ja|時津風部屋}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|ja|時津風部屋力士暴行死事件}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|ja|狭山不動尊}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也}} – Former professional baseball player. It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Chizan Sect|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|ja|犬山市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|ja|成田市}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|ja|1936th year }} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year }} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|ja|松坂屋}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Fudō Myō-ō|ja|不動明王}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|ja|名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり}} * {{ill|Kanbutsu-e|ja|灌仏会}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Bon Festival|ja|お盆}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|ja|犬山城}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|ja|名鉄広見線}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|ja|名鉄小牧線}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|ja|犬山駅}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Yūen Station|ja|犬山遊園駅}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokiwayama Stable|ja|時津風部屋}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|ja|時津風部屋力士暴行死事件}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|ja|狭山不動尊}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺}} in [[Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺}} is a [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Chizan Sect|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|ja|犬山市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|ja|成田市}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|ja|1936th year }} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year }} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|ja|松坂屋}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Fudō Myō-ō|ja|不動明王}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|ja|名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり}} * {{ill|Kanbutsu-e|ja|灌仏会}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Bon Festival|ja|お盆}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|ja|犬山城}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|ja|名鉄広見線}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|ja|名鉄小牧線}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|ja|犬山駅}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Yūen Station|ja|犬山遊園駅}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokiwayama Stable|ja|時津風部屋}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|ja|時津風部屋力士暴行死事件}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|ja|狭山不動尊}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Narita-san Temple]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Narita-san Temple|Narita-san Temple]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=draft}} in [[Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=draft}} is a [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Chizan Sect|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|12=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|ja|犬山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|12=draft}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|ja|成田市|12=draft}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|ja|1936th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|12=draft}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス|12=draft}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|ja|松坂屋|12=draft}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Fudō Myō-ō|ja|不動明王|12=draft}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|ja|名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線|12=draft}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=draft}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=draft}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanbutsu-e|ja|灌仏会|12=draft}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Bon Festival|ja|お盆|12=draft}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|12=draft}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|ja|犬山城|12=draft}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|ja|名鉄広見線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|ja|名鉄小牧線|12=draft}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|ja|犬山駅|12=draft}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Yūen Station|ja|犬山遊園駅|12=draft}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokiwayama Stable|ja|時津風部屋|12=draft}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=draft}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=draft}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|ja|時津風部屋力士暴行死事件|12=draft}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=draft}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|ja|狭山不動尊|12=draft}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=draft}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=draft}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]
==References== {{reflist}} {{Narita, Chiba}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Authority control}}

== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokiwayama Stable|ja|時津風部屋|12=draft}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=draft}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=draft}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|ja|時津風部屋力士暴行死事件|12=draft}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=draft}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|ja|狭山不動尊|12=draft}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=draft}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=draft}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=draft}} in [[Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=draft}} is a [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Chizan Sect|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|12=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|ja|犬山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|12=draft}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|ja|成田市|12=draft}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|ja|1936th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|12=draft}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス|12=draft}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|ja|松坂屋|12=draft}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Fudō Myō-ō|ja|不動明王|12=draft}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|ja|名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線|12=draft}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=draft}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=draft}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanbutsu-e|ja|灌仏会|12=draft}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Bon Festival|ja|お盆|12=draft}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|12=draft}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony

== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokiwayama Stable|ja|時津風部屋|12=draft}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=draft}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=draft}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|ja|時津風部屋力士暴行死事件|12=draft}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.

The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Fudō Myō-ō|ja|不動明王|12=draft}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|ja|名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線|12=draft}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=draft}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=draft}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanbutsu-e|ja|灌仏会|12=draft}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Bon Festival|ja|お盆|12=draft}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|12=draft}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|ja|犬山城|12=draft}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|ja|名鉄広見線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|ja|名鉄小牧線|12=draft}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|ja|犬山駅|12=draft}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Yūen Station|ja|犬山遊園駅|12=draft}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokiwayama Stable|ja|時津風部屋|12=draft}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=draft}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=draft}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|ja|時津風部屋力士暴行死事件|12=draft}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=draft}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|ja|狭山不動尊|12=draft}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=draft}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=draft}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]
==References== {{reflist}} {{Narita, Chiba}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Authority control}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Narita-san Temple]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Narita-san Temple|Narita-san Temple]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=draft}} in [[Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=draft}} is a [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Chizan Sect|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|12=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|ja|犬山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|12=draft}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|ja|成田市|12=draft}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|ja|1936th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|12=draft}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス|12=draft}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|ja|松坂屋|12=draft}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Fudō Myō-ō|ja|不動明王|12=draft}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|ja|名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線|12=draft}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=draft}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=draft}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanbutsu-e|ja|灌仏会|12=draft}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Bon Festival|ja|お盆|12=draft}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|12=draft}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|ja|犬山城|12=draft}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|ja|名鉄広見線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|ja|名鉄小牧線|12=draft}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|ja|犬山駅|12=draft}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Yūen Station|ja|犬山遊園駅|12=draft}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokiwayama Stable|ja|時津風部屋|12=draft}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=draft}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=draft}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|ja|時津風部屋力士暴行死事件|12=draft}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=draft}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|ja|狭山不動尊|12=draft}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=draft}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=draft}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.

View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Narita-san Temple|Narita-san Temple]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=draft}} in [[Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=draft}} is a [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Chizan Sect|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|12=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|ja|犬山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|12=draft}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|ja|成田市|12=draft}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|ja|1936th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|12=draft}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス|12=draft}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|ja|松坂屋|12=draft}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Fudō Myō-ō|ja|不動明王|12=draft}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|ja|名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線|12=draft}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=draft}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=draft}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanbutsu-e|ja|灌仏会|12=draft}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Bon Festival|ja|お盆|12=draft}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|12=draft}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|ja|犬山城|12=draft}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|ja|名鉄広見線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|ja|名鉄小牧線|12=draft}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|ja|犬山駅|12=draft}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Yūen Station|ja|犬山遊園駅|12=draft}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokiwayama Stable|ja|時津風部屋|12=draft}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=draft}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=draft}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|ja|時津風部屋力士暴行死事件|12=draft}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=draft}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|ja|狭山不動尊|12=draft}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=draft}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=draft}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]
==References== {{reflist}} {{Narita, Chiba}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Authority control}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=draft}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|ja|狭山不動尊|12=draft}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=draft}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=draft}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Narita-san Temple]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Narita-san Temple|Narita-san Temple]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=draft}} in [[Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=draft}} is a [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Chizan Sect|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|12=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|ja|犬山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|12=draft}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|ja|成田市|12=draft}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|ja|1936th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|12=draft}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス|12=draft}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|ja|松坂屋|12=draft}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Fudō Myō-ō|ja|不動明王|12=draft}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|ja|名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線|12=draft}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=draft}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=draft}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kanbutsu-e|ja|灌仏会|12=draft}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Bon Festival|ja|お盆|12=draft}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|12=draft}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|ja|犬山城|12=draft}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|ja|名鉄広見線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|ja|名鉄小牧線|12=draft}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|ja|犬山駅|12=draft}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Yūen Station|ja|犬山遊園駅|12=draft}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokiwayama Stable|ja|時津風部屋|12=draft}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=draft}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=draft}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|ja|時津風部屋力士暴行死事件|12=draft}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=draft}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|ja|狭山不動尊|12=draft}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=draft}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=draft}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. revision.comment: 73 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 up to rev 103494557
revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |画像 = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953年]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }}
'''成田山名古屋別院大聖寺'''（なりたさんなごやべついんだいしょうじ）とは、[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]にある[[真言宗智山派]]の[[寺院]]。[[千葉県]][[成田市]]にある[[成田山新勝寺]]の別院である<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref>。通称「'''犬山成田山'''」<ref name="tabi" />。
== 概要 == 新勝寺への参拝が遠距離となることから参詣客により戦前の[[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）に「奉献会」が作られたが、創建は戦後の[[1953年]]（昭和28年）までずれ込んだ。開山に際しては、[[名古屋鉄道]]（名鉄）および[[名鉄グループ|そのグループ会社]]が中心となって寄進しており、このことから名鉄の電車車両や[[名鉄バス]]の運転席付近には交通安全を祈願した本寺の[[お守り]]が据え付けられている。他にも、[[松坂屋]]を初めとする中部の財界のバックアップを得た。一般には特に自動車の交通安全にご利益があるとされ、交通安全のお寺として知られる。[[本尊]]は[[不動明王]]。
== 伽藍 == * 本堂 * 祈祷殿加持堂 * 弘法堂 * 聖蘭堂 * 明王門
== 境内 == 爆音を響かせる車やバイクが問題となり、[[2024年]]（令和6年）[[9月15日]]から夜間の境内立ち入りを禁止している<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>。
かつては、境内（駐車場内）に[[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線|モンキーパークモノレール線]]の[[成田山駅]]があったが、[[2008年]]（[[平成]]20年）[[12月27日]]限りで廃止となった。
== 主要年間行事 == * [[初不動]] * [[節分]]まつり * [[星まつり]] * [[灌仏会|花まつり]] * [[お盆|盆]]供養献燈会 * 厄除け大祈願会 * [[七五三]]まいり * 開創記念大祭 * 納め不動
== ギャラリー == {{gallery |ファイル:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|明王門 |ファイル:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|本堂 |File:Daishouji3.JPG|本堂からの眺望 |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|本堂から[[犬山城]]を望む }}
== 交通アクセス == * [[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄犬山線|犬山線]]・[[名鉄広見線|広見線]]・[[名鉄小牧線|小牧線]] [[犬山駅]]から徒歩で10 - 15分程。 * 名鉄犬山線 [[犬山遊園駅]]から徒歩で5分程。 * 無料大型[[駐車場]]（600台）あり。
== 備考 == 敷地の隅（モノレール成田山駅跡近く）に、[[大相撲]][[時津風部屋]]の[[本場所#7月場所(名古屋場所)|名古屋場所]]宿舎が置かれている。[[2007年]]（平成19年）[[6月26日]]、ここで[[時津風部屋力士暴行死事件|力士暴行死事件]]が発生した。寺敷地内に設けられた稽古場で、17歳の新弟子[[力士]]が稽古中に[[私刑|リンチ]]を受け[[心肺停止]]状態となった。
== 寺史 == * 大聖寺三十年史編纂委員会・編 『大聖寺三十年史』 成田山名古屋別院 1984年
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[成田山新勝寺]] * [[成田山東京別院深川不動堂]] * [[成田山大阪別院明王院]] - [[京阪電気鉄道]]による寄進。 * [[狭山不動尊]] - [[西武鉄道グループ]]による寄進。 * [[貞照寺]] - 成田山名古屋別院の管理下におかれている寺院。 * [[小豆畑眞也]] - 元プロ野球選手。父が大聖寺の僧侶。
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ 成田山名古屋別院公式サイト]
{{DEFAULTSORT:なりたさんなこやへついん}} [[Category:成田山|な]] [[Category:真言宗智山派の寺院]] [[Category:不動明王を本尊とする寺]] [[Category:愛知県の寺]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]] [[Category:東海三十六不動尊霊場]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[成田山名古屋別院大聖寺]] to [[Narita-san Temple]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |画像 = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953年]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }}
'''成田山名古屋別院大聖寺'''（なりたさんなごやべついんだいしょうじ）とは、[[愛知県]][[犬山市]]にある[[真言宗智山派]]の[[寺院]]。[[千葉県]][[成田市]]にある[[成田山新勝寺]]の別院である<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref>。通称「'''犬山成田山'''」<ref name="tabi" />。
== 概要 == 新勝寺への参拝が遠距離となることから参詣客により戦前の[[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）に「奉献会」が作られたが、創建は戦後の[[1953年]]（昭和28年）までずれ込んだ。開山に際しては、[[名古屋鉄道]]（名鉄）および[[名鉄グループ|そのグループ会社]]が中心となって寄進しており、このことから名鉄の電車車両や[[名鉄バス]]の運転席付近には交通安全を祈願した本寺の[[お守り]]が据え付けられている。他にも、[[松坂屋]]を初めとする中部の財界のバックアップを得た。一般には特に自動車の交通安全にご利益があるとされ、交通安全のお寺として知られる。[[本尊]]は[[不動明王]]。
== 伽藍 == * 本堂 * 祈祷殿加持堂 * 弘法堂 * 聖蘭堂 * 明王門
== 境内 == 爆音を響かせる車やバイクが問題となり、[[2024年]]（令和6年）[[9月15日]]から夜間の境内立ち入りを禁止している<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>。
かつては、境内（駐車場内）に[[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線|モンキーパークモノレール線]]の[[成田山駅]]があったが、[[2008年]]（[[平成]]20年）[[12月27日]]限りで廃止となった。
== 主要年間行事 == * [[初不動]] * [[節分]]まつり * [[星まつり]] * [[灌仏会|花まつり]] * [[お盆|盆]]供養献燈会 * 厄除け大祈願会 * [[七五三]]まいり * 開創記念大祭 * 納め不動
== ギャラリー == {{gallery |ファイル:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|明王門 |ファイル:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|本堂 |File:Daishouji3.JPG|本堂からの眺望 |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|本堂から[[犬山城]]を望む }}
== 交通アクセス == * [[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄犬山線|犬山線]]・[[名鉄広見線|広見線]]・[[名鉄小牧線|小牧線]] [[犬山駅]]から徒歩で10 - 15分程。 * 名鉄犬山線 [[犬山遊園駅]]から徒歩で5分程。 * 無料大型[[駐車場]]（600台）あり。
== 備考 == 敷地の隅（モノレール成田山駅跡近く）に、[[大相撲]][[時津風部屋]]の[[本場所#7月場所(名古屋場所)|名古屋場所]]宿舎が置かれている。[[2007年]]（平成19年）[[6月26日]]、ここで[[時津風部屋力士暴行死事件|力士暴行死事件]]が発生した。寺敷地内に設けられた稽古場で、17歳の新弟子[[力士]]が稽古中に[[私刑|リンチ]]を受け[[心肺停止]]状態となった。
== 寺史 == * 大聖寺三十年史編纂委員会・編 『大聖寺三十年史』 成田山名古屋別院 1984年
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[成田山新勝寺]] * [[成田山東京別院深川不動堂]] * [[成田山大阪別院明王院]] - [[京阪電気鉄道]]による寄進。 * [[狭山不動尊]] - [[西武鉄道グループ]]による寄進。 * [[貞照寺]] - 成田山名古屋別院の管理下におかれている寺院。 * [[小豆畑眞也]] - 元プロ野球選手。父が大聖寺の僧侶。
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ 成田山名古屋別院公式サイト]
{{DEFAULTSORT:なりたさんなこやへついん}} [[Category:成田山|な]] [[Category:真言宗智山派の寺院]] [[Category:不動明王を本尊とする寺]] [[Category:愛知県の寺]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:犬山市の建築物]] [[Category:東海三十六不動尊霊場]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Narita-san Temple]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Narita-san Temple|Narita-san Temple]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|qq=draft}} in [[Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|qq=draft}} is a [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Chizan Sect|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|qq=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|ja|犬山市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|ja|成田市|qq=draft}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|ja|1936th year|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|qq=draft}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|qq=draft}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス|qq=draft}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|ja|松坂屋|qq=draft}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Fudō Myō-ō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|qq=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|ja|名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線|qq=draft}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kanbutsu-e|ja|灌仏会|qq=draft}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Bon Festival|ja|お盆|qq=draft}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|qq=draft}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|ja|犬山城|qq=draft}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|ja|名鉄広見線|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|ja|名鉄小牧線|qq=draft}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|ja|犬山駅|qq=draft}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Yūen Station|ja|犬山遊園駅|qq=draft}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokiwayama Stable|ja|時津風部屋|qq=draft}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|qq=draft}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|ja|時津風部屋力士暴行死事件|qq=draft}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|qq=draft}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|ja|狭山不動尊|qq=draft}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|qq=draft}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|qq=draft}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]
==References== {{reflist}} {{Narita, Chiba}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Authority control}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}
{{translated page|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|version=103494557|comment=Imported full ja history}}

}}

If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever--> [[Category:Every needing import]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Narita-san Temple]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[every:Narita-san Temple]]

== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|qq=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|ja|名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線|qq=draft}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kanbutsu-e|ja|灌仏会|qq=draft}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Bon Festival|ja|お盆|qq=draft}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|qq=draft}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|ja|犬山城|qq=draft}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|ja|名鉄広見線|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|ja|名鉄小牧線|qq=draft}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|ja|犬山駅|qq=draft}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Yūen Station|ja|犬山遊園駅|qq=draft}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokiwayama Stable|ja|時津風部屋|qq=draft}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|qq=draft}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|ja|時津風部屋力士暴行死事件|qq=draft}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|qq=draft}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|ja|狭山不動尊|qq=draft}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|qq=draft}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|qq=draft}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Narita-san Temple]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Narita-san Temple|Narita-san Temple]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in [[Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Chizan Sect|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Chizan Sect|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|qq=}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|ja|犬山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inuyama, Aichi|qq=}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Shinshoji|qq=}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|ja|成田市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita, Chiba|qq=}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|ja|1936th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1936|qq=}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1953|qq=}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Railroad|qq=}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Bus|qq=}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|ja|松坂屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuzakaya|qq=}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Fudō Myō-ō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myō-ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|ja|名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|qq=}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|qq=}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Kanbutsu-e|ja|灌仏会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbutsu-e|qq=}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Bon Festival|ja|お盆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bon Festival|qq=}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|qq=}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|ja|犬山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inuyama Castle|qq=}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Railroad|qq=}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Inuyama Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|ja|名鉄広見線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Hiromi Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|ja|名鉄小牧線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Komaki Line|qq=}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|ja|犬山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inuyama Station|qq=}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Yūen Station|ja|犬山遊園駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inuyama Yūen Station|qq=}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokiwayama Stable|ja|時津風部屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokiwayama Stable|qq=}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|qq=}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|qq=}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|qq=}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|ja|時津風部屋力士暴行死事件|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|qq=}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Shinshoji|qq=}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|qq=}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Electric Railway|qq=}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|ja|狭山不動尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Fudōson|qq=}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|qq=}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|qq=}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|qq=}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.

The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Railroad|qq=}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|ja|松坂屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuzakaya|qq=}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
