== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|ja|名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|qq=}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|qq=}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Kanbutsu-e|ja|灌仏会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbutsu-e|qq=}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Bon Festival|ja|お盆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bon Festival|qq=}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|qq=}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|ja|犬山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inuyama Castle|qq=}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Railroad|qq=}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Inuyama Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|ja|名鉄広見線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Hiromi Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|ja|名鉄小牧線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Komaki Line|qq=}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|ja|犬山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inuyama Station|qq=}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Yūen Station|ja|犬山遊園駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inuyama Yūen Station|qq=}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokiwayama Stable|ja|時津風部屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokiwayama Stable|qq=}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|qq=}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|qq=}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|qq=}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|ja|時津風部屋力士暴行死事件|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|qq=}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Shinshoji|qq=}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|qq=}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Electric Railway|qq=}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|ja|狭山不動尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Fudōson|qq=}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|qq=}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|qq=}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|qq=}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Narita-san Temple]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Narita-san Temple|Narita-san Temple]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in [[Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Chizan Sect|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Chizan Sect|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|qq=}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|ja|犬山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inuyama, Aichi|qq=}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Shinshoji|qq=}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|ja|成田市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita, Chiba|qq=}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|ja|1936th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1936|qq=}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1953|qq=}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Railroad|qq=}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Bus|qq=}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|ja|松坂屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuzakaya|qq=}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Fudō Myō-ō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myō-ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|ja|名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|qq=}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|qq=}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Kanbutsu-e|ja|灌仏会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbutsu-e|qq=}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Bon Festival|ja|お盆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bon Festival|qq=}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|qq=}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|ja|犬山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inuyama Castle|qq=}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Railroad|qq=}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Inuyama Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|ja|名鉄広見線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Hiromi Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|ja|名鉄小牧線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Komaki Line|qq=}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|ja|犬山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inuyama Station|qq=}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Yūen Station|ja|犬山遊園駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inuyama Yūen Station|qq=}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokiwayama Stable|ja|時津風部屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokiwayama Stable|qq=}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|qq=}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|qq=}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|qq=}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|ja|時津風部屋力士暴行死事件|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|qq=}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Shinshoji|qq=}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|qq=}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Electric Railway|qq=}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|ja|狭山不動尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Fudōson|qq=}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|qq=}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|qq=}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|qq=}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1953|qq=}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Railroad|qq=}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Bus|qq=}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|ja|松坂屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuzakaya|qq=}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Fudō Myō-ō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myō-ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|ja|名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|qq=}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|qq=}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Kanbutsu-e|ja|灌仏会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbutsu-e|qq=}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Bon Festival|ja|お盆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bon Festival|qq=}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|qq=}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|ja|犬山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inuyama Castle|qq=}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Railroad|qq=}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|ja|名鉄犬山線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Inuyama Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|ja|名鉄広見線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Hiromi Line|qq=}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|ja|名鉄小牧線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Komaki Line|qq=}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|ja|犬山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inuyama Station|qq=}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Yūen Station|ja|犬山遊園駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inuyama Yūen Station|qq=}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokiwayama Stable|ja|時津風部屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokiwayama Stable|qq=}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|qq=}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|qq=}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|qq=}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|ja|時津風部屋力士暴行死事件|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|qq=}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Shinshoji|qq=}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|qq=}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|ja|京阪電気鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Electric Railway|qq=}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|ja|狭山不動尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Fudōson|qq=}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|qq=}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|qq=}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|qq=}} – Former professional baseball player. {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Narita-san Temple]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Narita-san Temple|Narita-san Temple]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in [[Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Chizan Sect|ja|真言宗智山派|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Chizan Sect|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|qq=}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|ja|犬山市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inuyama, Aichi|qq=}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Naritasan Shinshoji|ja|成田山新勝寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Shinshoji|qq=}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|ja|成田市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita, Chiba|qq=}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|ja|1936年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1936|qq=}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|ja|1953年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1953|qq=}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Nagoya Railroad|ja|名古屋鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Railroad|qq=}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Bus|qq=}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|ja|松坂屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuzakaya|qq=}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Fudō Myō-ō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myō-ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|ja|名鉄モンキーパークモノレール線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|qq=}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|qq=}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Kanbutsu-e|ja|灌仏会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbutsu-e|qq=}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Bon Festival|ja|お盆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bon Festival|qq=}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|qq=}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|ja|犬山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inuyama Castle|qq=}} from the Main Hall }}

It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a [[:en:Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] temple located in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita, Chiba]].<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in [[:en:1936|1936]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]] vehicles. Additional financial support came from [[:en:Matsuzakaya|Matsuzakaya]] and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|qq=}} on the [[:en:Monkey Park Monorail Line|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line]], but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|qq=}} ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|qq=}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Buddha's Birthday|Kanbutsu-e]] (Flower Festival) * [[:en:Obon|Bon Festival]] Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of [[:en:Inuyama Castle|Inuyama Castle]] from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Meitetsu Inuyama Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Hiromi Line|Meitetsu Hiromi Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Komaki Line|Meitetsu Komaki Line]] - 10-15 minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama Station|Inuyama Station]]. * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama-Yūen Station|Inuyama Yūen Station]]. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable|Tokiwayama Stable]], associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|qq=}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|qq=}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|qq=}}, the [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal]] occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|qq=}} – Donated by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]]. * [[:en:Sayama Fudōson|Sayama Fudōson]] – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|qq=}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|qq=}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|qq=}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Narita-san Temple]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Narita-san Temple|Narita-san Temple]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in [[Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a [[:en:Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] temple located in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita, Chiba]].<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in [[:en:1936|1936]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]] vehicles. Additional financial support came from [[:en:Matsuzakaya|Matsuzakaya]] and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|qq=}} on the [[:en:Monkey Park Monorail Line|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line]], but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|qq=}} ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 20).

== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of [[:en:Inuyama Castle|Inuyama Castle]] from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Meitetsu Inuyama Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Hiromi Line|Meitetsu Hiromi Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Komaki Line|Meitetsu Komaki Line]] - 10-15 minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama Station|Inuyama Station]]. * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama-Yūen Station|Inuyama Yūen Station]]. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable|Tokiwayama Stable]], associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|qq=}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|qq=}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|qq=}}, the [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal]] occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Buildings and structures in Inuyama, Aichi
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Narita-san Temple]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Narita-san Temple|Narita-san Temple]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in [[Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a [[:en:Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] temple located in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita, Chiba]].<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in [[:en:1936|1936]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]] vehicles. Additional financial support came from [[:en:Matsuzakaya|Matsuzakaya]] and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|qq=}} on the [[:en:Monkey Park Monorail Line|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line]], but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|qq=}} ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|qq=}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Buddha's Birthday|Kanbutsu-e]] (Flower Festival) * [[:en:Obon|Bon Festival]] Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of [[:en:Inuyama Castle|Inuyama Castle]] from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Meitetsu Inuyama Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Hiromi Line|Meitetsu Hiromi Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Komaki Line|Meitetsu Komaki Line]] - 10-15 minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama Station|Inuyama Station]]. * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama-Yūen Station|Inuyama Yūen Station]]. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable|Tokiwayama Stable]], associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|qq=}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|qq=}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|qq=}}, the [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal]] occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|qq=}} – Donated by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]]. * [[:en:Sayama Fudōson|Sayama Fudōson]] – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|qq=}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|qq=}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|qq=}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]
==References== {{reflist}} {{Narita, Chiba}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Authority control}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}
{{translated page|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|version=103494557|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Temples in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Inuyama]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Inuyama]]

}}

If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever--> [[Category:Every needing import]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Narita-san Temple]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[every:Narita-san Temple]] [[Category:成田山]] [[Category:東海三十六不動尊霊場]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Inuyama, Aichi]]

His father is a monk at Daishoji.

== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|qq=}} on the [[:en:Monkey Park Monorail Line|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line]], but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|qq=}} ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|qq=}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Buddha's Birthday|Kanbutsu-e]] (Flower Festival) * [[:en:Obon|Bon Festival]] Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of [[:en:Inuyama Castle|Inuyama Castle]] from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Meitetsu Inuyama Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Hiromi Line|Meitetsu Hiromi Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Komaki Line|Meitetsu Komaki Line]] - 10-15 minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama Station|Inuyama Station]]. * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama-Yūen Station|Inuyama Yūen Station]]. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable|Tokiwayama Stable]], associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|qq=}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|qq=}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|qq=}}, the [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal]] occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|qq=}} – Donated by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]]. * [[:en:Sayama Fudōson|Sayama Fudōson]] – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|qq=}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|qq=}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|qq=}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.

{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Narita-san Temple]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Narita-san Temple|Narita-san Temple]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in [[Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a [[:en:Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] temple located in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita, Chiba]].<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in [[:en:1936|1936]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]] vehicles. Additional financial support came from [[:en:Matsuzakaya|Matsuzakaya]] and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|qq=}} on the [[:en:Monkey Park Monorail Line|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line]], but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|qq=}} ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|qq=}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Buddha's Birthday|Kanbutsu-e]] (Flower Festival) * [[:en:Obon|Bon Festival]] Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of [[:en:Inuyama Castle|Inuyama Castle]] from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Meitetsu Inuyama Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Hiromi Line|Meitetsu Hiromi Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Komaki Line|Meitetsu Komaki Line]] - 10-15 minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama Station|Inuyama Station]]. * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama-Yūen Station|Inuyama Yūen Station]]. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable|Tokiwayama Stable]], associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|qq=}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|qq=}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|qq=}}, the [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal]] occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|qq=}} – Donated by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]]. * [[:en:Sayama Fudōson|Sayama Fudōson]] – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|qq=}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|qq=}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|qq=}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|qq=}} – Donated by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]]. * [[:en:Sayama Fudōson|Sayama Fudōson]] – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|qq=}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|qq=}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Buildings and structures in Inuyama
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Narita-san Temple]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Narita-san Temple|Narita-san Temple]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in [[Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a [[:en:Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] temple located in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita, Chiba]].<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in [[:en:1936|1936]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]] vehicles. Additional financial support came from [[:en:Matsuzakaya|Matsuzakaya]] and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} on the [[:en:Monkey Park Monorail Line|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line]], but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Buddha's Birthday|Kanbutsu-e]] (Flower Festival) * [[:en:Obon|Bon Festival]] Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of [[:en:Inuyama Castle|Inuyama Castle]] from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Meitetsu Inuyama Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Hiromi Line|Meitetsu Hiromi Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Komaki Line|Meitetsu Komaki Line]] - 10-15 minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama Station|Inuyama Station]]. * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama-Yūen Station|Inuyama Yūen Station]]. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable|Tokiwayama Stable]], associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本場所}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|qq=}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal]] occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|qq=}} – Donated by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]]. * [[:en:Sayama Fudōson|Sayama Fudōson]] – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|qq=}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|qq=}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]
==References== {{reflist}} {{Narita, Chiba}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Authority control}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} <!--removing this template because now we do not need it. All everybodywiki edit histories have been imported-->

}}

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in [[Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a [[Buddhist temple]] in [[Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a [[:en:Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] temple located in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita, Chiba]].<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in [[:en:1936|1936]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]] vehicles. Additional financial support came from [[:en:Matsuzakaya|Matsuzakaya]] and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} on the [[:en:Monkey Park Monorail Line|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line]], but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Buddha's Birthday|Kanbutsu-e]] (Flower Festival) * [[:en:Obon|Bon Festival]] Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of [[:en:Inuyama Castle|Inuyama Castle]] from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Meitetsu Inuyama Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Hiromi Line|Meitetsu Hiromi Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Komaki Line|Meitetsu Komaki Line]] - 10-15 minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama Station|Inuyama Station]]. * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama-Yūen Station|Inuyama Yūen Station]]. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable|Tokiwayama Stable]], associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本場所}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|qq=}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal]] occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|qq=}} – Donated by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]]. * [[:en:Sayama Fudōson|Sayama Fudōson]] – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|qq=}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|qq=}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} on the [[:en:Monkey Park Monorail Line|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line]], but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Buddha's Birthday|Kanbutsu-e]] (Flower Festival) * [[:en:Obon|Bon Festival]] Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of [[:en:Inuyama Castle|Inuyama Castle]] from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Meitetsu Inuyama Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Hiromi Line|Meitetsu Hiromi Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Komaki Line|Meitetsu Komaki Line]] - 10-15 minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama Station|Inuyama Station]]. * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama-Yūen Station|Inuyama Yūen Station]]. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable|Tokiwayama Stable]], associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本場所}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|qq=}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal]] occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|qq=}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|qq=}} – Donated by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]]. * [[:en:Sayama Fudōson|Sayama Fudōson]] – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|qq=}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|qq=}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]
