<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[en:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[ja:成田山名古屋別院大聖寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:View of Larger Daishoji complex.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Inuyama|WD=Q503411}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.3891|N|136.949231|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Acala|WD=Q337624}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11495646]] Wikidata name: Daishō-ji (Inuyama) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Narita-san Temple]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Narita-san Temple|Narita-san Temple]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temples in Japan]] in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a [[:en:Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] temple located in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita, Chiba]].<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in [[:en:1936|1936]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]] vehicles. Additional financial support came from [[:en:Matsuzakaya|Matsuzakaya]] and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Naritasan Station|WD=Q6965992}} on the [[:en:Monkey Park Monorail Line|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line]], but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日|1=Ennichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985655}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Buddha's Birthday|Kanbutsu-e]] (Flower Festival) * [[:en:Obon|Bon Festival]] Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of [[:en:Inuyama Castle|Inuyama Castle]] from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Meitetsu Inuyama Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Hiromi Line|Meitetsu Hiromi Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Komaki Line|Meitetsu Komaki Line]] - 10-15 minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama Station|Inuyama Station]]. * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama-Yūen Station|Inuyama Yūen Station]]. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable|Tokiwayama Stable]], associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本場所|1=honbasho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q835803}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|1=Ōzumō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10937013}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal]] occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|1=Fukagawa Fudōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495650}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|1=Naritasan Osaka betsuin Myooin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495647}} – Donated by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]]. * [[:en:Sayama Fudōson|Sayama Fudōson]] – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ|1=Seibu Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628875}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|1=Teishoji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634533}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|1=Shin'ya Azuhata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463804}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日|1=Ennichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985655}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Buddha's Birthday|Kanbutsu-e]] (Flower Festival) * [[:en:Obon|Bon Festival]] Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[en:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[ja:成田山名古屋別院大聖寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:View of Larger Daishoji complex.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Inuyama|WD=Q503411}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.3891|N|136.949231|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Acala|WD=Q337624}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11495646]] Wikidata name: Daishō-ji (Inuyama) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. {{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temples in Japan]] in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a [[:en:Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] temple located in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita, Chiba]].<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in [[:en:1936|1936]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]] vehicles. Additional financial support came from [[:en:Matsuzakaya|Matsuzakaya]] and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Naritasan Station|WD=Q6965992}} on the [[:en:Monkey Park Monorail Line|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line]], but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日|1=Ennichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985655}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Buddha's Birthday|Kanbutsu-e]] (Flower Festival) * [[:en:Obon|Bon Festival]] Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of [[:en:Inuyama Castle|Inuyama Castle]] from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Meitetsu Inuyama Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Hiromi Line|Meitetsu Hiromi Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Komaki Line|Meitetsu Komaki Line]] - 10-15 minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama Station|Inuyama Station]]. * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama-Yūen Station|Inuyama Yūen Station]]. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable|Tokiwayama Stable]], associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本場所|1=honbasho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q835803}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|1=Ōzumō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10937013}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal]] occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|1=Fukagawa Fudōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495650}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|1=Naritasan Osaka betsuin Myooin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495647}} – Donated by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]]. * [[:en:Sayama Fudōson|Sayama Fudōson]] – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ|1=Seibu Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628875}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|1=Teishoji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634533}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|1=Shin'ya Azuhata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463804}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temples in Japan]] in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a [[:en:Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] temple located in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita, Chiba]].<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in [[:en:1936|1936]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]] vehicles. Additional financial support came from [[:en:Matsuzakaya|Matsuzakaya]] and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Naritasan Station|WD=Q6965992}} on the [[:en:Monkey Park Monorail Line|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line]], but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日|1=Ennichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985655}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Buddha's Birthday|Kanbutsu-e]] (Flower Festival) * [[:en:Obon|Bon Festival]] Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of [[:en:Inuyama Castle|Inuyama Castle]] from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Meitetsu Inuyama Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Hiromi Line|Meitetsu Hiromi Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Komaki Line|Meitetsu Komaki Line]] - 10-15 minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama Station|Inuyama Station]]. * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama-Yūen Station|Inuyama Yūen Station]]. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable|Tokiwayama Stable]], associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本場所|1=honbasho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q835803}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|1=Ōzumō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10937013}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal]] occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|1=Fukagawa Fudōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495650}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|1=Naritasan Osaka betsuin Myooin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495647}} – Donated by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]]. * [[:en:Sayama Fudōson|Sayama Fudōson]] – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ|1=Seibu Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628875}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|1=Teishoji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634533}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|1=Shin'ya Azuhata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463804}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita, Chiba]].<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />

The founding of the temple was primarily supported by [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]] vehicles. Additional financial support came from [[:en:Matsuzakaya|Matsuzakaya]] and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Naritasan Station|WD=Q6965992}} on the [[:en:Monkey Park Monorail Line|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line]], but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日|1=Ennichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985655}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Buddha's Birthday|Kanbutsu-e]] (Flower Festival) * [[:en:Obon|Bon Festival]] Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony

== Notes == A training facility for [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable|Tokiwayama Stable]], associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本場所|1=honbasho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q835803}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|1=Ōzumō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10937013}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. His father is a monk at Daishoji.

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temples in Japan]] in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a [[:en:Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] temple located in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita, Chiba]].<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in [[:en:1936|1936]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]] vehicles. Additional financial support came from [[:en:Matsuzakaya|Matsuzakaya]] and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Naritasan Station|WD=Q6965992}} on the [[:en:Monkey Park Monorail Line|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line]], but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日|1=Ennichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985655}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Buddha's Birthday|Kanbutsu-e]] (Flower Festival) * [[:en:Obon|Bon Festival]] Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of [[:en:Inuyama Castle|Inuyama Castle]] from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Meitetsu Inuyama Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Hiromi Line|Meitetsu Hiromi Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Komaki Line|Meitetsu Komaki Line]] - 10-15 minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama Station|Inuyama Station]]. * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama-Yūen Station|Inuyama Yūen Station]]. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable|Tokiwayama Stable]], associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本場所|1=honbasho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q835803}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|1=Ōzumō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10937013}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal]] occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|1=Fukagawa Fudōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495650}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|1=Naritasan Osaka betsuin Myooin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495647}} – Donated by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]]. * [[:en:Sayama Fudōson|Sayama Fudōson]] – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ|1=Seibu Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628875}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|1=Teishoji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634533}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|1=Shin'ya Azuhata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463804}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temples in Japan]] in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a [[:en:Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] temple located in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita, Chiba]].<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in [[:en:1936|1936]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]] vehicles. Additional financial support came from [[:en:Matsuzakaya|Matsuzakaya]] and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Naritasan Station|WD=Q6965992}} on the [[:en:Monkey Park Monorail Line|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line]], but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日|1=Ennichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985655}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Buddha's Birthday|Kanbutsu-e]] (Flower Festival) * [[:en:Obon|Bon Festival]] Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of [[:en:Inuyama Castle|Inuyama Castle]] from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Meitetsu Inuyama Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Hiromi Line|Meitetsu Hiromi Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Komaki Line|Meitetsu Komaki Line]] - 10-15 minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama Station|Inuyama Station]]. * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama-Yūen Station|Inuyama Yūen Station]]. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable|Tokiwayama Stable]], associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本場所|1=honbasho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q835803}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|1=Ōzumō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10937013}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal]] occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|1=Fukagawa Fudōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495650}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|1=Naritasan Osaka betsuin Myooin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495647}} – Donated by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]]. * [[:en:Sayama Fudōson|Sayama Fudōson]] – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ|1=Seibu Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628875}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|1=Teishoji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634533}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|1=Shin'ya Azuhata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463804}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temples in Japan]] in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a [[:en:Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] temple located in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita, Chiba]].<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in [[:en:1936|1936]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]] vehicles. Additional financial support came from [[:en:Matsuzakaya|Matsuzakaya]] and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Naritasan Station|WD=Q6965992}} on the [[:en:Monkey Park Monorail Line|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line]], but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日|1=Ennichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985655}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Buddha's Birthday|Kanbutsu-e]] (Flower Festival) * [[:en:Obon|Bon Festival]] Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of [[:en:Inuyama Castle|Inuyama Castle]] from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Meitetsu Inuyama Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Hiromi Line|Meitetsu Hiromi Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Komaki Line|Meitetsu Komaki Line]] - 10-15 minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama Station|Inuyama Station]]. * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama-Yūen Station|Inuyama Yūen Station]]. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable|Tokiwayama Stable]], associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本場所|1=honbasho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q835803}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|1=Ōzumō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10937013}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal]] occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|1=Fukagawa Fudōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495650}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|1=Naritasan Osaka betsuin Myooin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495647}} – Donated by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]]. * [[:en:Sayama Fudōson|Sayama Fudōson]] – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ|1=Seibu Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628875}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|1=Teishoji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634533}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|1=Shin'ya Azuhata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463804}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]]]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|Buddhist temples in Japan]] in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]].
It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Narita-san]] '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a [[:en:Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha]] temple located in [[:en:Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a branch temple of [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita, Chiba]].<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in [[:en:1936|1936]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until [[:en:1953|1953]] (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]] vehicles. Additional financial support came from [[:en:Matsuzakaya|Matsuzakaya]] and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]].
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Naritasan Station|WD=Q6965992}} on the [[:en:Monkey Park Monorail Line|Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line]], but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日|1=Ennichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985655}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Buddha's Birthday|Kanbutsu-e]] (Flower Festival) * [[:en:Obon|Bon Festival]] Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of [[:en:Inuyama Castle|Inuyama Castle]] from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * [[:en:Meitetsu|Nagoya Railroad]] [[:en:Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Meitetsu Inuyama Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Hiromi Line|Meitetsu Hiromi Line]], [[:en:Meitetsu Komaki Line|Meitetsu Komaki Line]] - 10-15 minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama Station|Inuyama Station]]. * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Inuyama-Yūen Station|Inuyama Yūen Station]]. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable|Tokiwayama Stable]], associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本場所|1=honbasho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q835803}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|1=Ōzumō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10937013}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the [[:en:Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal]] occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * [[:en:Narita-san|Naritasan Shinshoji]] * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|1=Fukagawa Fudōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495650}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|1=Naritasan Osaka betsuin Myooin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495647}} – Donated by [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]]. * [[:en:Sayama Fudōson|Sayama Fudōson]] – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ|1=Seibu Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628875}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|1=Teishoji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634533}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|1=Shin'ya Azuhata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463804}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''{{ill|Hatsumōde|en|Hatsumōde|WD=Q1032381|lt=Hatsumōde}}'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi}}]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temples in Japan}} in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi}}.
It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Narita-san}} '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|en|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|WD=Q674374|lt=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|en|Narita, Chiba|WD=Q273798|lt=Narita, Chiba}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Nagoya Railroad}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|en|Meitetsu Bus|WD=Q6810397|lt=Meitetsu Bus}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|en|Matsuzakaya|WD=Q3116938|lt=Matsuzakaya}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Naritasan Station|WD=Q6965992}} on the {{ill|Monkey Park Monorail Line|en|Monkey Park Monorail Line|WD=Q7476121|lt=Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日|1=Ennichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985655}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Buddha's Birthday|en|Buddha's Birthday|WD=Q5348941|lt=Kanbutsu-e}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Obon|en|Obon|WD=Q1154888|lt=Bon Festival}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|en|Inuyama Castle|WD=Q1155484|lt=Inuyama Castle}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Nagoya Railroad}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|en|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|WD=Q6810411|lt=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|en|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|WD=Q6810410|lt=Meitetsu Hiromi Line}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|en|Meitetsu Komaki Line|WD=Q6788786|lt=Meitetsu Komaki Line}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|en|Inuyama Station|WD=Q6059394|lt=Inuyama Station}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama-Yūen Station|en|Inuyama-Yūen Station|WD=Q6059388|lt=Inuyama Yūen Station}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokitsukaze stable|en|Tokitsukaze stable|WD=Q7813610|lt=Tokiwayama Stable}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本場所|1=honbasho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q835803}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|1=Ōzumō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10937013}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|en|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|WD=Q7813615|lt=Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|1=Fukagawa Fudōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495650}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|1=Naritasan Osaka betsuin Myooin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495647}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|en|Sayama Fudōson|WD=Q11571731|lt=Sayama Fudōson}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ|1=Seibu Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628875}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|1=Teishoji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634533}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|1=Shin'ya Azuhata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463804}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''{{ill|Hatsumōde|en|Hatsumōde|WD=Q1032381|lt=Hatsumōde}}'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi}}]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temples in Japan}} in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi}}.
It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Narita-san}} '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|en|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|WD=Q674374|lt=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|en|Narita, Chiba|WD=Q273798|lt=Narita, Chiba}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Nagoya Railroad}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|en|Meitetsu Bus|WD=Q6810397|lt=Meitetsu Bus}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|en|Matsuzakaya|WD=Q3116938|lt=Matsuzakaya}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Naritasan Station|WD=Q6965992}} on the {{ill|Monkey Park Monorail Line|en|Monkey Park Monorail Line|WD=Q7476121|lt=Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日|1=Ennichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985655}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Buddha's Birthday|en|Buddha's Birthday|WD=Q5348941|lt=Kanbutsu-e}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Obon|en|Obon|WD=Q1154888|lt=Bon Festival}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|en|Inuyama Castle|WD=Q1155484|lt=Inuyama Castle}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Nagoya Railroad}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|en|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|WD=Q6810411|lt=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|en|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|WD=Q6810410|lt=Meitetsu Hiromi Line}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|en|Meitetsu Komaki Line|WD=Q6788786|lt=Meitetsu Komaki Line}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|en|Inuyama Station|WD=Q6059394|lt=Inuyama Station}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama-Yūen Station|en|Inuyama-Yūen Station|WD=Q6059388|lt=Inuyama Yūen Station}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokitsukaze stable|en|Tokitsukaze stable|WD=Q7813610|lt=Tokiwayama Stable}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本場所|1=honbasho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q835803}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|1=Ōzumō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10937013}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|en|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|WD=Q7813615|lt=Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|1=Fukagawa Fudōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495650}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|1=Naritasan Osaka betsuin Myooin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495647}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|en|Sayama Fudōson|WD=Q11571731|lt=Sayama Fudōson}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ|1=Seibu Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628875}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|1=Teishoji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634533}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|1=Shin'ya Azuhata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463804}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[en:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[ja:成田山名古屋別院大聖寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[en:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[ja:成田山名古屋別院大聖寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[en:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[ja:成田山名古屋別院大聖寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:View of Larger Daishoji complex.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Inuyama|WD=Q503411}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.3891|N|136.949231|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Acala|WD=Q337624}}}}
{{afc comment|1=to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Narita-san Temple]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Narita-san Temple|Narita-san Temple]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''{{ill|Hatsumōde|en|Hatsumōde|WD=Q1032381|lt=Hatsumōde}}'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi}}]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temples in Japan}} in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi}}.
It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Narita-san}} '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|en|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|WD=Q674374|lt=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|en|Narita, Chiba|WD=Q273798|lt=Narita, Chiba}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Nagoya Railroad}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|en|Meitetsu Bus|WD=Q6810397|lt=Meitetsu Bus}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|en|Matsuzakaya|WD=Q3116938|lt=Matsuzakaya}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Naritasan Station|WD=Q6965992}} on the {{ill|Monkey Park Monorail Line|en|Monkey Park Monorail Line|WD=Q7476121|lt=Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日|1=Ennichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985655}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Buddha's Birthday|en|Buddha's Birthday|WD=Q5348941|lt=Kanbutsu-e}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Obon|en|Obon|WD=Q1154888|lt=Bon Festival}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|en|Inuyama Castle|WD=Q1155484|lt=Inuyama Castle}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Nagoya Railroad}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|en|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|WD=Q6810411|lt=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|en|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|WD=Q6810410|lt=Meitetsu Hiromi Line}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|en|Meitetsu Komaki Line|WD=Q6788786|lt=Meitetsu Komaki Line}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|en|Inuyama Station|WD=Q6059394|lt=Inuyama Station}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama-Yūen Station|en|Inuyama-Yūen Station|WD=Q6059388|lt=Inuyama Yūen Station}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokitsukaze stable|en|Tokitsukaze stable|WD=Q7813610|lt=Tokiwayama Stable}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本場所|1=honbasho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q835803}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|1=Ōzumō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10937013}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|en|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|WD=Q7813615|lt=Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|1=Fukagawa Fudōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495650}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|1=Naritasan Osaka betsuin Myooin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495647}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|en|Sayama Fudōson|WD=Q11571731|lt=Sayama Fudōson}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ|1=Seibu Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628875}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|1=Teishoji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634533}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|1=Shin'ya Azuhata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463804}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]
