On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|en|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|WD=Q7813615|lt=Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|1=Fukagawa Fudōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495650}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|1=Naritasan Osaka betsuin Myooin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495647}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|en|Sayama Fudōson|WD=Q11571731|lt=Sayama Fudōson}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ|1=Seibu Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628875}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|1=Teishoji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634533}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|1=Shin'ya Azuhata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463804}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[en:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[ja:成田山名古屋別院大聖寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[en:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[ja:成田山名古屋別院大聖寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[en:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[ja:成田山名古屋別院大聖寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:View of Larger Daishoji complex.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Inuyama|WD=Q503411}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.3891|N|136.949231|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Acala|WD=Q337624}}}}
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''{{ill|Hatsumōde|en|Hatsumōde|WD=Q1032381|lt=Hatsumōde}}'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi}}]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temples in Japan}} in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi}}.
It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Narita-san}} '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|en|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|WD=Q674374|lt=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|en|Narita, Chiba|WD=Q273798|lt=Narita, Chiba}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Nagoya Railroad}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|en|Meitetsu Bus|WD=Q6810397|lt=Meitetsu Bus}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|en|Matsuzakaya|WD=Q3116938|lt=Matsuzakaya}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Naritasan Station|WD=Q6965992}} on the {{ill|Monkey Park Monorail Line|en|Monkey Park Monorail Line|WD=Q7476121|lt=Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日|1=Ennichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985655}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Buddha's Birthday|en|Buddha's Birthday|WD=Q5348941|lt=Kanbutsu-e}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Obon|en|Obon|WD=Q1154888|lt=Bon Festival}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|en|Inuyama Castle|WD=Q1155484|lt=Inuyama Castle}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Nagoya Railroad}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|en|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|WD=Q6810411|lt=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|en|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|WD=Q6810410|lt=Meitetsu Hiromi Line}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|en|Meitetsu Komaki Line|WD=Q6788786|lt=Meitetsu Komaki Line}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|en|Inuyama Station|WD=Q6059394|lt=Inuyama Station}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama-Yūen Station|en|Inuyama-Yūen Station|WD=Q6059388|lt=Inuyama Yūen Station}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokitsukaze stable|en|Tokitsukaze stable|WD=Q7813610|lt=Tokiwayama Stable}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本場所|1=honbasho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q835803}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|1=Ōzumō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10937013}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|en|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|WD=Q7813615|lt=Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|1=Fukagawa Fudōson|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495650}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|1=Naritasan Osaka betsuin Myooin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495647}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|en|Sayama Fudōson|WD=Q11571731|lt=Sayama Fudōson}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ|1=Seibu Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628875}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|1=Teishoji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634533}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|1=Shin'ya Azuhata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463804}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji|1=Narita-san}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|en|Narita, Chiba|WD=Q273798|lt=Narita, Chiba|1=Narita, Chiba}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936|1=1936}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Nagoya Railroad|1=Meitetsu}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|en|Meitetsu Bus|WD=Q6810397|lt=Meitetsu Bus|1=Meitetsu Bus}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|en|Matsuzakaya|WD=Q3116938|lt=Matsuzakaya|1=Matsuzakaya}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Naritasan Station|WD=Q6965992}} on the {{ill|Monkey Park Monorail Line|en|Monkey Park Monorail Line|WD=Q7476121|lt=Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|1=Monkey Park Monorail Line}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日|1=Ennichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985655}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Buddha's Birthday|en|Buddha's Birthday|WD=Q5348941|lt=Kanbutsu-e|1=Buddha's Birthday}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Obon|en|Obon|WD=Q1154888|lt=Bon Festival|1=Obon}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San|1=Shichi-Go-San}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony

== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Nagoya Railroad|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|en|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|WD=Q6810411|lt=Meitetsu Inuyama Line|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|en|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|WD=Q6810410|lt=Meitetsu Hiromi Line|1=Meitetsu Hiromi Line}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|en|Meitetsu Komaki Line|WD=Q6788786|lt=Meitetsu Komaki Line|1=Meitetsu Komaki Line}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|en|Inuyama Station|WD=Q6059394|lt=Inuyama Station|1=Inuyama Station}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama-Yūen Station|en|Inuyama-Yūen Station|WD=Q6059388|lt=Inuyama Yūen Station|1=Inuyama-Yūen Station}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).

On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|en|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|WD=Q7813615|lt=Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|1=Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[en:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[ja:成田山名古屋別院大聖寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[en:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[ja:成田山名古屋別院大聖寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[en:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[ja:成田山名古屋別院大聖寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:View of Larger Daishoji complex.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Inuyama|WD=Q503411|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.3891|N|136.949231|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Acala|WD=Q337624|1=Acala}}}}
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''{{ill|Hatsumōde|en|Hatsumōde|WD=Q1032381|lt=Hatsumōde|1=Hatsumōde}}'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temples in Japan|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}} in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}.
It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Narita-san|1=Narita-san}} '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|en|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|WD=Q674374|lt=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji|1=Narita-san}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|en|Narita, Chiba|WD=Q273798|lt=Narita, Chiba|1=Narita, Chiba}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936|1=1936}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Nagoya Railroad|1=Meitetsu}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|en|Meitetsu Bus|WD=Q6810397|lt=Meitetsu Bus|1=Meitetsu Bus}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|en|Matsuzakaya|WD=Q3116938|lt=Matsuzakaya|1=Matsuzakaya}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Naritasan Station|WD=Q6965992}} on the {{ill|Monkey Park Monorail Line|en|Monkey Park Monorail Line|WD=Q7476121|lt=Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|1=Monkey Park Monorail Line}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 20).

The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Naritasan Station|WD=Q6965992}} on the {{ill|Monkey Park Monorail Line|en|Monkey Park Monorail Line|WD=Q7476121|lt=Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|1=Monkey Park Monorail Line}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 20).

On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|en|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|WD=Q7813615|lt=Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|1=Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[en:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[ja:成田山名古屋別院大聖寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[en:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[ja:成田山名古屋別院大聖寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[en:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[ja:成田山名古屋別院大聖寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:View of Larger Daishoji complex.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Inuyama|WD=Q503411|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.3891|N|136.949231|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Acala|WD=Q337624|1=Acala}}}}
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''{{ill|Hatsumōde|en|Hatsumōde|WD=Q1032381|lt=Hatsumōde|1=Hatsumōde}}'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temples in Japan|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}} in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}.
It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Narita-san|1=Narita-san}} '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|en|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|WD=Q674374|lt=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji|1=Narita-san}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|en|Narita, Chiba|WD=Q273798|lt=Narita, Chiba|1=Narita, Chiba}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936|1=1936}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Nagoya Railroad|1=Meitetsu}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|en|Meitetsu Bus|WD=Q6810397|lt=Meitetsu Bus|1=Meitetsu Bus}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|en|Matsuzakaya|WD=Q3116938|lt=Matsuzakaya|1=Matsuzakaya}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}.

It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Narita-san|1=Narita-san}} '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|en|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|WD=Q674374|lt=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji|1=Narita-san}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|en|Narita, Chiba|WD=Q273798|lt=Narita, Chiba|1=Narita, Chiba}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936|1=1936}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Nagoya Railroad|1=Meitetsu}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|en|Meitetsu Bus|WD=Q6810397|lt=Meitetsu Bus|1=Meitetsu Bus}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|en|Matsuzakaya|WD=Q3116938|lt=Matsuzakaya|1=Matsuzakaya}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Naritasan Station|WD=Q6965992}} on the {{ill|Monkey Park Monorail Line|en|Monkey Park Monorail Line|WD=Q7476121|lt=Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|1=Monkey Park Monorail Line}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日|1=Ennichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985655}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Buddha's Birthday|en|Buddha's Birthday|WD=Q5348941|lt=Kanbutsu-e|1=Buddha's Birthday}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Obon|en|Obon|WD=Q1154888|lt=Bon Festival|1=Obon}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San|1=Shichi-Go-San}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|en|Inuyama Castle|WD=Q1155484|lt=Inuyama Castle|1=Inuyama Castle}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Nagoya Railroad|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|en|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|WD=Q6810411|lt=Meitetsu Inuyama Line|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|en|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|WD=Q6810410|lt=Meitetsu Hiromi Line|1=Meitetsu Hiromi Line}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|en|Meitetsu Komaki Line|WD=Q6788786|lt=Meitetsu Komaki Line|1=Meitetsu Komaki Line}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|en|Inuyama Station|WD=Q6059394|lt=Inuyama Station|1=Inuyama Station}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama-Yūen Station|en|Inuyama-Yūen Station|WD=Q6059388|lt=Inuyama Yūen Station|1=Inuyama-Yūen Station}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokitsukaze stable|en|Tokitsukaze stable|WD=Q7813610|lt=Tokiwayama Stable|1=Tokitsukaze stable}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本場所|1=Honbasho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q835803}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|1=Ōzumō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10937013}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|en|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|WD=Q7813615|lt=Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|1=Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji|1=Narita-san}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|1=Fukagawa Fudō-dō Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495650}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|1=Naritasan Osaka betsuin Myoo-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495647}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|1=Keihan Electric Railway}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|en|Sayama Fudōson|WD=Q11571731|lt=Sayama Fudōson|1=Sayama Fudōson}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ|1=Seibu Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628875}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|1=Teisho-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634533}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|1=Shin'ya Azuhata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463804}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[en:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[ja:成田山名古屋別院大聖寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[en:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[ja:成田山名古屋別院大聖寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[en:Daishō-ji (Inuyama)]][[ja:成田山名古屋別院大聖寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:View of Larger Daishoji complex.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Inuyama|WD=Q503411|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.3891|N|136.949231|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Acala|WD=Q337624|1=Acala}}}}
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''{{ill|Hatsumōde|en|Hatsumōde|WD=Q1032381|lt=Hatsumōde|1=Hatsumōde}}'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temples in Japan|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}} in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}.
It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Narita-san|1=Narita-san}} '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|en|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|WD=Q674374|lt=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji|1=Narita-san}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|en|Narita, Chiba|WD=Q273798|lt=Narita, Chiba|1=Narita, Chiba}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936|1=1936}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Nagoya Railroad|1=Meitetsu}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|en|Meitetsu Bus|WD=Q6810397|lt=Meitetsu Bus|1=Meitetsu Bus}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|en|Matsuzakaya|WD=Q3116938|lt=Matsuzakaya|1=Matsuzakaya}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Naritasan Station|WD=Q6965992}} on the {{ill|Monkey Park Monorail Line|en|Monkey Park Monorail Line|WD=Q7476121|lt=Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|1=Monkey Park Monorail Line}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日|1=Ennichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985655}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Buddha's Birthday|en|Buddha's Birthday|WD=Q5348941|lt=Kanbutsu-e|1=Buddha's Birthday}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Obon|en|Obon|WD=Q1154888|lt=Bon Festival|1=Obon}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San|1=Shichi-Go-San}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|en|Inuyama Castle|WD=Q1155484|lt=Inuyama Castle|1=Inuyama Castle}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Nagoya Railroad|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|en|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|WD=Q6810411|lt=Meitetsu Inuyama Line|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|en|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|WD=Q6810410|lt=Meitetsu Hiromi Line|1=Meitetsu Hiromi Line}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|en|Meitetsu Komaki Line|WD=Q6788786|lt=Meitetsu Komaki Line|1=Meitetsu Komaki Line}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|en|Inuyama Station|WD=Q6059394|lt=Inuyama Station|1=Inuyama Station}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama-Yūen Station|en|Inuyama-Yūen Station|WD=Q6059388|lt=Inuyama Yūen Station|1=Inuyama-Yūen Station}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokitsukaze stable|en|Tokitsukaze stable|WD=Q7813610|lt=Tokiwayama Stable|1=Tokitsukaze stable}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本場所|1=Honbasho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q835803}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|1=Ōzumō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10937013}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|en|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|WD=Q7813615|lt=Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|1=Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji|1=Narita-san}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|1=Fukagawa Fudō-dō Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495650}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|1=Naritasan Osaka betsuin Myoo-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495647}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|1=Keihan Electric Railway}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|en|Sayama Fudōson|WD=Q11571731|lt=Sayama Fudōson|1=Sayama Fudōson}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ|1=Seibu Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628875}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|1=Teisho-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634533}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|1=Shin'ya Azuhata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463804}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Daishoji (Inuyama)}} * [http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ Official Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Website]

On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|en|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|WD=Q7813615|lt=Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|1=Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." revision.text:
{{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:View of Larger Daishoji complex.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Inuyama|WD=Q503411|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.3891|N|136.949231|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Acala|WD=Q337624|1=Acala}}}}
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 成田山名古屋別院大聖寺 |image = [[File:Partial view of Daishoji Staircase.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 愛知県犬山市犬山北白山平5番地 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 23|緯度秒 = 20.76 | 経度度 = 136|経度分 = 56|経度秒 = 57.23 |地図 = Japan#Japan Aichi |山号 = 成田山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = [[真言宗智山派]] |寺格 = |本尊 = [[不動明王]] |創建年 = [[1953]] |開山 = |開基 = |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.inuyama-naritasan.or.jp/ |公式HP名 = 犬山成田山 }} [[File:Hatsumōde in Inuyama Naritasan No - 4.jpg|thumb|250px|''{{ill|Hatsumōde|en|Hatsumōde|WD=Q1032381|lt=Hatsumōde|1=Hatsumōde}}'' at {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}]] {{ill|Narita-san Temple|ja|成田山名古屋別院大聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita-san Temple|qq=}} is a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temples in Japan|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}} in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}.
It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Narita-san|1=Narita-san}} '''Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji''' (成田山名古屋別院大聖寺) is a {{ill|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|en|Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|WD=Q674374|lt=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha|1=Shingon-shū Chisan-ha}} temple located in {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|WD=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a branch temple of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji|1=Narita-san}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|en|Narita, Chiba|WD=Q273798|lt=Narita, Chiba|1=Narita, Chiba}}.<ref name="tabi">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://inuyama-tabi.com/data/download/100/8/inuyamahigashi.pdf |title=犬山たび 巻之二 犬山東編 |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=犬山市教育委員会|page=15}}</ref> Commonly referred to as "'''Inuyama Naritasan'''".<ref name="tabi" />
== Overview == A "Dedication Association" was formed in {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936|1=1936}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 11) due to the long distance required for pilgrimage to Shinshoji. However, the temple was not officially established until {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}} (Shōwa 28) after World War II.
The founding of the temple was primarily supported by {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Nagoya Railroad|1=Meitetsu}} (Meitetsu) and its group companies, which contributed donations. Due to this association, amulets from the temple, praying for traffic safety, are placed near the driver's seat in Meitetsu trains and {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|en|Meitetsu Bus|WD=Q6810397|lt=Meitetsu Bus|1=Meitetsu Bus}} vehicles. Additional financial support came from {{ill|Matsuzakaya|en|Matsuzakaya|WD=Q3116938|lt=Matsuzakaya|1=Matsuzakaya}} and other business leaders in central Japan.
The temple is especially known for granting blessings for traffic safety and is commonly referred to as a "traffic safety temple." The principal deity is {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}.
== Temple Complex == * Main Hall * Prayer Hall (Kaji-do) * Kōbō Hall * Seiran Hall * Myōō Gate
== Grounds == Due to disturbances caused by loud vehicles and motorcycles, entry into the temple grounds at night has been prohibited since {{ill|September 15, 2024|ja|2024th year 9月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 15, 2024|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/957659 |title=境内に爆音車続々…「犬山成田山」が苦渋の決断、夜間の立ち入り禁止へ |access-date=2024-9-16 |publisher=中日新聞}}</ref>
The temple grounds previously housed {{ill|Naritasan Station|ja|成田山駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Naritasan Station|WD=Q6965992}} on the {{ill|Monkey Park Monorail Line|en|Monkey Park Monorail Line|WD=Q7476121|lt=Meitetsu Monkey Park Monorail Line|1=Monkey Park Monorail Line}}, but the station was closed on {{ill|December 27, 2008|ja|2008th year 12月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 27, 2008|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 20).
== Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Hatsu Fudō|ja|初不動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsu Fudō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 縁日|1=Ennichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985655}} * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Star Festival|ja|星まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Buddha's Birthday|en|Buddha's Birthday|WD=Q5348941|lt=Kanbutsu-e|1=Buddha's Birthday}} (Flower Festival) * {{ill|Obon|en|Obon|WD=Q1154888|lt=Bon Festival|1=Obon}} Memorial Lantern Dedication * Great Exorcism Prayer Ceremony * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San|1=Shichi-Go-San}} Celebration * Founding Anniversary Grand Festival * Year-End Fudō Ceremony
== Gallery == {{gallery |File:View of Daishoji while climing the staircase.jpg|Myōō Gate |File:Inuyama Naritasan Hondō.jpg|Main Hall |File:Daishouji3.JPG|View from the Main Hall |File:Inuyama Castle from daishoji.jpg|View of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|en|Inuyama Castle|WD=Q1155484|lt=Inuyama Castle|1=Inuyama Castle}} from the Main Hall }}
== Access == * {{ill|Meitetsu|en|Meitetsu|WD=Q30850|lt=Nagoya Railroad|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|en|Meitetsu Inuyama Line|WD=Q6810411|lt=Meitetsu Inuyama Line|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|en|Meitetsu Hiromi Line|WD=Q6810410|lt=Meitetsu Hiromi Line|1=Meitetsu Hiromi Line}}, {{ill|Meitetsu Komaki Line|en|Meitetsu Komaki Line|WD=Q6788786|lt=Meitetsu Komaki Line|1=Meitetsu Komaki Line}} - 10-15 minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama Station|en|Inuyama Station|WD=Q6059394|lt=Inuyama Station|1=Inuyama Station}}. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Inuyama-Yūen Station|en|Inuyama-Yūen Station|WD=Q6059388|lt=Inuyama Yūen Station|1=Inuyama-Yūen Station}}. * Free large parking lot (600 cars available).
== Notes == A training facility for {{ill|Tokitsukaze stable|en|Tokitsukaze stable|WD=Q7813610|lt=Tokiwayama Stable|1=Tokitsukaze stable}}, associated with the {{ill|Nagoya Tournament|ja|名古屋場所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoya Tournament|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本場所|1=Honbasho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q835803}} of {{ill|Professional Sumo|ja|大相撲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Professional Sumo|1=Ōzumō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10937013}}, is located on the temple grounds near the former Naritasan Station site. On {{ill|June 26, 2007|ja|2007th year 6月26日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 26, 2007|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|en|Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal|WD=Q7813615|lt=Tokitsukaze Stable Scandal|1=Tokitsukaze stable hazing scandal}} occurred here, where a 17-year-old sumo apprentice suffered fatal injuries during training.
== Temple History == * Daishoji 30-Year History Compilation Committee (1984). "Daishoji 30-Year History." Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. == See Also == * {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji|1=Narita-san}} * {{ill|Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|ja|成田山東京別院深川不動堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Tokyo Betsuin Fukagawa Fudōdō|1=Fukagawa Fudō-dō Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495650}} * {{ill|Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|ja|成田山大阪別院明王院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naritasan Osaka Betsuin Myōō-in|1=Naritasan Osaka betsuin Myoo-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495647}} – Donated by {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|1=Keihan Electric Railway}}. * {{ill|Sayama Fudōson|en|Sayama Fudōson|WD=Q11571731|lt=Sayama Fudōson|1=Sayama Fudōson}} – Donated by {{ill|Seibu Railway Group|ja|西武鉄道グループ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibu Railway Group|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西武グループ|1=Seibu Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11628875}}. * {{ill|Teishō-ji|ja|貞照寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teishō-ji|1=Teisho-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634533}} – Managed under Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin. * {{ill|Shinya Azukibata|ja|小豆畑眞也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinya Azukibata|1=Shin'ya Azuhata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463804}} – Former professional baseball player. His father is a monk at Daishoji.
